name: jsonl-real-sink-unpadded-image-rejected
comment: The regression this whole real-sink case class exists for. 'abc' is malformed base64; Node's decoder used to accept it leniently while Python's raised, so this exact entry produced an 'image' ContentPart in TS and an 'unknown' one in Python - a wire-visible divergence the stub-sink corpus could not see, because nothing at that layer decoded. Both ports now validate before decoding and both land on the unknown part below.
